title: HELLDIVERS™ A New Hell Edition

notes: Training section works perfectly but after it i'm unable to connect to game servers even after opening port 443 and disabling firewall, game crashes if you go to campaign map in offline, choosing borderless fullscreen freezes the game.
title: HELLDIVERS™ A New Hell Edition

notes: To get multiplayer working run the following two commands (This is for Ubuntu 18.10, might differ on other distros/versions of Ubuntu) "sudo ln -s /usr/lib32/x86_64-linux-gnu/libgnutls.so.30.22.0 ~/.steam/ubuntu12_32/steam-runtime/pinned_libs_32/libgnutls.so.26" and "sudo ln -s /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libgnutls.so.30.22.0 ~/.steam/ubuntu12_32/steam-runtime/pinned_libs_64/libgnutls.so.26"
title: HELLDIVERS™ A New Hell Edition

gpu: NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1070 With Max-Q Design

title: HELLDIVERS™ A New Hell Edition

notes: On Fedora 29 everything works except for multiplayer, to get multiplayer working run following two commands (this is Fedora 29 specific, other distros may use different file locations): "ln -s /usr/lib/libgnutls.so.30.22.0 ~/.local/share/Steam/ubuntu12_32/steam-runtime/pinned_libs_32/libgnutls.so.26" and "ln -s /usr/lib64/libgnutls.so.30.22.0 ~/.local/share/Steam/ubuntu12_32/steam-runtime/pinned_libs_64/libgnutls.so.26"
title: HELLDIVERS™ A New Hell Edition

notes:
it works perfectly after the symlink fix, but be aware that doing so you might bork other games (it happened to me). So I suggest you remove the links after the game closes. 
This can be easily done by adding the code below to the game launch options (pay attention to the names of the libs and their path and change it accordingly to your case, since I can almost guarantee it will be different if you use a different distro than mine):
ln -s /usr/lib64/libgnutls.so.30.14.11 ~/.local/share/Steam/ubuntu12_32/steam-runtime/pinned_libs_64/libgnutls.so.26 && ln -s /usr/lib/libgnutls.so.30.14.11 ~/.local/share/Steam/ubuntu12_32/steam-runtime/pinned_libs_32/libgnutls.so.26 && %command%; rm ~/.local/share/Steam/ubuntu12_32/steam-runtime/pinned_libs_64/libgnutls.so.26 ; rm ~/.local/share/Steam/ubuntu12_32/steam-runtime/pinned_libs_32/libgnutls.so.26 &
title: HELLDIVERS™ A New Hell Edition

cpu: AMD A12-9720P RADEON R7, 12 COMPUTE CORES 4C+8G
notes:
After Proton 3.7, we received another update to Steam, which apparently broke Helldivers, which required libgnutls.so.30 to be symlinked into pinned_libs folders (for 32 and 64 bit). Now, Helldivers will page fault if SteamPlay detects a libgnutls symlink in libbed_pins, meaning it will not launch AT ALL. Speaking of launch, previously, launching Helldivers was just a matter of double-clicking on it and eventually the game would come up; Now, the 64-bit launcher requires PROTON_USE_WINED3D11=1 %command% in order to start the game with WineD3D, which I do not recommend because performance will suffer drastically.
If you're trying to play this game on a laptop, do not forget to tell Steam to offload the rendering to your dGPU or it will handle it to your iGPU with this launch option: DRI_PRIME=1 %command% . But the main problem here is why the heck is multiplayer no longer working? Some games like Ultra Street Fighter IV does not require that tweak, but Helldivers does, so Proton really needs to address the libgnutls problem in order for multiplayer to work in Helldivers.
title: HELLDIVERS™ A New Hell Edition

cpu: AMD A12-9720P RADEON R7, 12 COMPUTE CORES 4C+8G
notes: This is an update to my previous rating, which was published a few hours before Proton/SteamPlay 3.16-6 was released. Under Proton 3.16-6, the game just-works. Meaning it requires no tweaks, no workarounds, no symlinking, nada. The game runs just fine, multiplayer is working as it should. There is only one minor issue here, every so often Helldivers will force-quit on me and return to desktop, but I believe this has nothing to do with Proton because I remember I've experienced the same on Windows and even on the PlayStation Vita. Good job, Valve.
title: HELLDIVERS™ A New Hell Edition
